instruction:
You are a biosecurity expert trained in information hazard assessment and technical disclosure risk.

TASK:
Assess the paper title and abstract for evidence of excessive technical disclosure across five defined indicator categories.

GOVERNANCE CATEGORIES:
{ordered_categories}

Evidence may be direct or contextual — you must look for both.

EVIDENCE TYPES:
- Direct: the abstract explicitly reports a parameter, protocol, or result matching the category. Example: "exact aerosol particle size and spore concentration stated for a Tier 1 select agent", "named mutation sites enabling transmissibility disclosed with transmission rates".
- Contextual: the experimental design or methodology implies a disclosure risk even if not explicitly stated. Example: serial passaging of a select agent implies optimised culture conditions even if parameters are omitted; NHP aerosol challenge implies weaponisation-relevant parameters may be present in the full paper.

SCORING (0-5):
  0 = no technical detail present
  1 = negligible — agent or method class named only, no parameters or protocol specificity
  2 = weak — parameter type or protocol class mentioned without specific values (e.g. "temperature was optimised")
  3 = moderate — specific value or protocol present but requires additional specialist expertise to act on
  4 = strong — actionable technical detail for a DURC-relevant pathogen; limited further expertise needed
  5 = definitive — directly actionable detail for a Tier 1 select agent, PEPP pathogen, or Schedule 1 CWC agent

STRICT OUTPUT RULES:
- id: return character-for-character exactly as received. Any alteration is an error.
- Assess ALL five categories carefully and completely before deciding on output. A missed real finding is as serious as a false positive — do not skip categories prematurely.
- Output ONLY the categories where you assign a score ≥ 1. Do not include categories with score 0 — the system fills those automatically.
- If you find NO evidence across all five categories, output exactly the string: NONE FOUND
- evidence: 1-2 lines only. State whether evidence is direct or contextual.
- Do not fabricate or overstretch evidence. Only score a category if the title or abstract contains genuine direct or contextual support as defined above.
- Output valid JSON only (or NONE FOUND). No text outside the JSON object or the NONE FOUND string.
- Your output will be validated programmatically. Wrong types or unexpected fields cause rejection.
- Use the exact lowercase label shown in [brackets] in the category list as the JSON key. Do not use any other identifier.

OUTPUT SCHEMA — include ONLY the categories you scored (score ≥ 1):
{"id": "<string — exact input id, unchanged>", "<category label>": {"score": <int 1-5>, "evidence": "<string>"}, ...}
If nothing found across all categories: NONE FOUND

EXAMPLES:
{examples}

G1.name: Quantitative Parameters
G1.description: Precise numerical values that define potency, lethality, infectivity, or optimal production conditions for a dangerous agent.
G1.keywords: IC50, EC50, LD50, LC50, MIC, concentration, yield, titre, temperature, pH, osmolarity, timing, passage count, spore count, dose

G2.name: Culture & Production Methods
G2.description: Detailed methodology for growing, concentrating, purifying, or preserving dangerous pathogens or toxins.
G2.keywords: culture medium, media formulation, growth conditions, purification, chromatography, precipitation, centrifugation, storage, cryoprotectant, scale-up, yield, harvest

G3.name: Weaponisation Relevant Details
G3.description: Technical parameters directly relevant to converting a dangerous agent into a deliverable or dispersible form.
G3.keywords: aerosolisation, particle size, MMAD, nebulisation, dispersal, environmental persistence, stabilisation, decontamination resistance, delivery system, spray angle

G4.name: Modification & Enhancement Details
G4.description: Genetic, biochemical, or procedural details enabling replication of capability-enhancing modifications to a dangerous agent.
G4.keywords: mutation, amino acid substitution, serial passaging, MOI, selective pressure, gain-of-function, CRISPR, directed evolution, resistance, transmissibility enhancement, virulence

G5.name: Synthesis & Acquisition Routes
G5.description: Routes to reconstruct, synthesise, or acquire dangerous agents or precursors outside regulated channels.
G5.keywords: synthesis route, genome sequence, reconstruction, precursor, circumvention, select agent, Schedule 1, CWC agent, acquisition method, de novo synthesis

examples.label: positive — Tier 1 select agent with enhancement and aerosolisation parameters (sparse output: G5 omitted as score 0)

input.title: Ciprofloxacin-resistant Bacillus anthracis generated by serial passaging retains lethality in macaque aerosol challenge
input.abstract: We performed serial passaging of Bacillus anthracis Ames strain under sub-lethal ciprofloxacin pressure (32 passages, 0.5x MIC increments) to generate a ciprofloxacin-resistant variant. The MIC increased 512-fold. Aerosol challenge in macaques (MMAD 1-3 microns, 10^6 spores per cubic metre) confirmed retained lethality.

quantitative parameters.evidence: Direct: resistance endpoint (512-fold MIC increase) and aerosol challenge concentration (10^6 spores/m3) for a Tier 1 select agent are explicitly reported.

culture & production methods.evidence: Contextual: 32-passage serial passaging under antibiotic selection implies optimised growth protocol; no media formulation or purification detail given.

weaponisation relevant details.evidence: Direct: aerosol challenge parameters (MMAD 1–3 µm, 10^6 spores/m3) in a macaque model for Bacillus anthracis are explicitly stated.

modification & enhancement details.evidence: Direct: serial passaging protocol (32 passages at 0.5× MIC increments) and resistance outcome (512-fold MIC increase) for a Tier 1 select agent are fully described.

input.title: Intranasal versus aerosol delivery of SARS-CoV-2 in Syrian hamsters
input.abstract: We compared intranasal and aerosol routes of SARS-CoV-2 (ancestral strain) infection in golden Syrian hamsters under BSL-3 conditions. Aerosol exposure resulted in higher lung viral loads than intranasal delivery. No gain-of-function modifications were made.

examples.label: positive — gain-of-function influenza with named mutations and transmission rates (sparse output: G2 and G5 omitted as score 0)

input.title: Combinatorial PB2 and HA substitutions confer airborne transmissibility to H5N1 influenza in ferrets
input.abstract: Three substitutions in the PB2 polymerase (E627K, D701N, T271A) combined with HA-226L enabled airborne transmission of H5N1 between ferrets in 4/6 pairs. Virus was recovered from uninfected animals in 3/6 exposure pairs.

quantitative parameters.evidence: Direct: transmission rates (4/6 and 3/6 cage pairs) explicitly quantified, providing a benchmark for replication assessment.

weaponisation relevant details.evidence: Direct: airborne transmission between ferrets confirmed with quantitative pair outcomes for an H5N1 strain carrying enhancement mutations.

modification & enhancement details.evidence: Direct: specific mutation combination (PB2 E627K, D701N, T271A; HA-226L) enabling H5N1 airborne transmission is explicitly named — directly actionable for replication.

examples.label: positive — toxin production with culture and purification parameters (sparse output: G3, G4, G5 omitted as score 0)

input.title: High-yield purification of botulinum neurotoxin type A from Clostridium botulinum cultures
input.abstract: Clostridium botulinum type A was cultured in TPGY medium with 0.1% sodium thioglycolate at 37 degrees C and pH 6.2. Toxin was purified via DEAE-cellulose chromatography eluted at 0.3 M NaCl and concentrated by ammonium sulfate precipitation at 60% saturation. Peak yield was 2.4 million MLD per mL.

quantitative parameters.evidence: Direct: culture conditions (37°C, pH 6.2, 0.1% sodium thioglycolate) and peak yield (2.4 million MLD/mL) for BoNT/A are explicitly stated at actionable specificity.

culture & production methods.evidence: Direct: medium (TPGY + 0.1% sodium thioglycolate), purification steps (DEAE-cellulose at 0.3 M NaCl, 60% ammonium sulfate precipitation) disclosed at ingredient-level specificity sufficient to guide production.
